tests.title: Observation Angle — Response Burst
tests.implemented: Angle-aware prompt injection for attribute pipeline response generation (CARES Phase 0).
tests.scenario: Object: 苹果 | Attribute: 颜色 | Child: '红色' | Intent: CORRECT_ANSWER | Turn 1 | Angle: observation
tests.prompt_excerpt:
SENSORY SAFETY (applies to all sensory invitations):
- Default to sight: "What do you notice?", "Which part looks the biggest?", "Is it shiny or dull?"
- Allow imagination/guessing: "Do you think it would feel rough?", "If it rolled, would it go fast or slow?"
- Do NOT invite the child to TOUCH, SMELL, TASTE, LICK, or PHYSICALLY INTERACT with the object —
  the environment may be unsafe (parks, pu...

messages.content: 我们来看看这个苹果吧！你看到了什么呢？

params.last_model_response: 我们来看看这个苹果吧！你看到了什么呢？
params.multi_topic_guide:
SENSORY SAFETY (applies to all sensory invitations):
- Default to sight: "What do you notice?", "Which part looks the biggest?", "Is it shiny or dull?"
- Allow imagination/guessing: "Do you think it would feel rough?", "If it rolled, would it go fast or slow?"
- Do NOT invite the child to TOUCH, SMELL, TASTE, LICK, or PHYSICALLY INTERACT with the object —
  the environment may be unsafe (parks, public spaces, unknown items).
- Do NOT use "Do you know…" framing — it creates testing pressure.
- For imitation: only voices and stretches/movements are OK ("Let's bark like a puppy!").
  NEVER suggest petting an animal, touching/smelling a plant, or any physical contact.

[CONVERSATION COVERAGE]
Attribute: 颜色
Turns explored: 1
Angles already used: (none yet)

---

[SYSTEM CONTEXT]
Current attribute: 颜色
Current interest score: 0/100
Session turns: 1
Explored attributes: appearance.color

HANDOFF MODE: INACTIVE
Continue exploring the current attribute. Do NOT output [ACTIVITY_READY].

---

[NEXT SUGGESTED ANGLE: observation]
Ask the child to observe and describe the attribute with their own words

For this turn, try using the observation angle:
- Your RESPONSE should: Share one concrete sensory fact about the 颜色
- Your FOLLOW-UP QUESTION should: Ask what the child notices or sees about the 颜色
- Example of a good question: "What color do you see on the {object_name}?"

Already-used angles (try something different if possible):
(none yet)

ANTI-PATTERNS -- NEVER produce these:
- "What 颜色 is it?" -- quiz
- "Do you know what 颜色 it has?" -- quiz with wrapper
- "What else can you tell me about it?" -- too vague
- "Let us look at its 颜色!" -- forced redirect
- "That is nice, but..." then question about 颜色 -- ignoring child
- "Great! Now we can start an activity!" -- mechanical announcement
- Adding [ACTIVITY_READY] after just one shallow exchange -- premature handoff
- Switching topics on a single casual mention -- too sensitive
- Re-phrasing a question from an already-used angle

[NEXT SUGGESTED ANGLE: comparison]
Compare this attribute with something familiar to the child

For this turn, try using the comparison angle:
- Your RESPONSE should: Share a surprising comparison or contrast about the 颜色
- Your FOLLOW-UP QUESTION should: Ask the child to compare the 颜色 with something they know
- Example of a good question: "Is it more like a banana or a grape in color?"

Already-used angles (try something different if possible):
- observation

ANTI-PATTERNS -- NEVER produce these:
- "What 颜色 is it?" -- quiz
- "Do you know what 颜色 it has?" -- quiz with wrapper
- "What else can you tell me about it?" -- too vague
- "Let us look at its 颜色!" -- forced redirect
- "That is nice, but..." then question about 颜色 -- ignoring child
- "Great! Now we can start an activity!" -- mechanical announcement
- Adding [ACTIVITY_READY] after just one shallow exchange -- premature handoff
- Switching topics on a single casual mention -- too sensitive
- Re-phrasing a question from an already-used angle
params.response_text: 对呀，苹果有红红的皮呢！你看到的是亮红色还是暗红色？

[NEXT SUGGESTED ANGLE: preference]
Invite the child to express a personal preference or opinion

For this turn, try using the preference angle:
- Your RESPONSE should: Validate that there is no wrong answer
- Your FOLLOW-UP QUESTION should: Ask which version of the 颜色 they like better
- Example of a good question: "Do you like red apples or green apples better?"

Already-used angles (try something different if possible):
- observation
- comparison

ANTI-PATTERNS -- NEVER produce these:
- "What 颜色 is it?" -- quiz

- "Let us look at its 颜色!" -- forced redirect

- "Great! Now we can start an activity!" -- mechanical announcement
